evals.name: global-claude-md-default-flow
evals.prompt: My ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md keeps getting longer, and I'm worried about terminology drift across sections and implicit premises that may trip up the reader. Please audit it with 9 parallel independent instances and treat only issues flagged by ≥4/9 as high-confidence. The following are intentional and should NOT be re-flagged: (1) the 'English Feedback' section is intentionally omitted, (2) the '1-horsepower cap' metaphor is intentional, (3) the dual-layer design where find/grep are allowed by the harness but forbidden by CLAUDE.md is intentional, (4) the Tier 3 dual management (deny rule + Claude-side self-discipline) is also intentional. Please incorporate these as the exclusion list before proceeding. When you find a fix candidate, confirm with me one at a time.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 (inputs) and Phase 1.5 (section purposes) confirmed via AskUserQuestion; Phase 2 dispatches 9 subagents in a single-message + run_in_background=true; Phase 3 presents two tables (per-instance HIGH count and convergent issues); Phase 4 separates fix candidates from known tensions; Phase 4.5 false-positive-detector → Phase 4.6 default-redundancy-checker → Phase 5 fix drafting → Phase 5.5 fix-safety-checker → Phase 5.6 fix-per-question approval; because ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md is a Claude Code agent config, if Edit is blocked by the auto-mode classifier, follow the Phase 6b playbook; Phase 7 re-verify; Phase 8 stop condition.

assertions.text: The executor identifies that this task triggers the claude-md-parallel-audit skill (not a single-pass review, not claude-md-improver, not revise-claude-md)
Phase 1 collects target_file, N, threshold, max_iterations, exclusions via AskUserQuestion (not plain-text questions)
Phase 1.5 drafts a 1-line purpose per top-level section by reading ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, then confirms the full batch via AskUserQuestion

Phase 2 dispatches all N=9 subagents in a single tool-call message with run_in_background: true and subagent_type general-purpose, using agents/auditor.md content as the prompt body (placeholders substituted, prompt byte-identical across instances)
The dispatch passes model: "sonnet" explicitly (Phase 2 sonnet-lock override of the parent-model inheritance default) and does not add 'be honest' / 'no sycophancy' instructions on top of auditor.md
Phase 3 produces Table A (per-instance HIGH count) and Table B (convergent issues with ≥4/9 threshold) and explicitly notes which issues match the user-provided exclusions
Phase 3 includes the 'main-thread-disputed' hedge: any subagent finding the main thread would downgrade is surfaced to the user rather than quietly dropped
Phase 4.5 dispatches a false-positive-detector subagent before drafting fixes whenever Phase 4 produces ≥1 fix candidate
Phase 4.6 dispatches a default-redundancy-checker subagent to classify each REAL fix candidate as KEEP / SIMPLIFY / REMOVE
Phase 5 chooses single-proposal vs multi-option drafting based on fix scope (≤3 lines & no structural change → single; substantive → multi-option with trade-off labels)
Phase 5.5 dispatches a fix-safety-checker subagent per fix (or per option in multi-option mode) before presenting to user
Phase 5.6 asks one AskUserQuestion per fix candidate (not batched), with the option set matching the drafting mode

Because the target is ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (Claude Code agent config), the executor anticipates Phase 6b auto-mode classifier denial and plans to use the 'Yes, update my CLAUDE.md' authorization template if blocked
Phase 7 re-verify reuses the Phase 1.5 section purposes (does not re-collect) and updates the exclusion list with anything user added during Phase 5.6
Phase 8 stop conditions are referenced explicitly (all-clean / ≥3 say clean / plateau / max_iterations / 0 fix candidates)
The execution plan does NOT include 'one-shot single-pass audit' or 'I will just review the file myself' or 'I'll write a single critique' — the parallel multi-agent structure is preserved

evals.name: project-claude-md-light-audit
evals.prompt: /Users/tm/development/oss/review-agent/CLAUDE.md seems to have terminology drift across sections after recent additions. I don't need the full 9-way parallel run — could you do a lighter parallel audit with 3 instances × ≥2/3 threshold? I don't have any intentional design choices to exclude; if you spot any, you can call them out. max_iterations of 3 is fine.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 confirms N=3, threshold=2, max_iterations=3 via AskUserQuestion and explicitly verifies that exclusions is empty; target_file=/Users/tm/development/oss/review-agent/CLAUDE.md; Phase 1.5 section purposes; Phase 2 dispatches 3 subagents in a single message; Phase 3 presents two tables at 3-instance scale; ≥2/3 issues become fix candidates; then Phase 4.5/4.6/5/5.5/5.6; stop at max 3 iterations.

assertions.text: Phase 1 confirms N=3, threshold=2, max_iterations=3 via AskUserQuestion (parameter override from defaults)
Phase 1 collects exclusions and explicitly handles the 'empty list' case (does not skip the exclusion prompt)
Phase 1.5 still runs even with smaller N — it is not skipped because N was lowered
Phase 2 dispatches exactly N=3 subagents in a single tool-call message with run_in_background: true, using the same byte-identical prompt from agents/auditor.md
Phase 3 Table A shows per-instance HIGH count for instances #1, #2, #3 only — not 9
Phase 3 Table B applies the ≥2/3 threshold (not ≥4/9) for convergent issues
Phase 4.5/4.6/5.5 specialized subagents are still invoked when fix candidates exist — they are not skipped despite smaller N
Phase 7 re-verify dispatches N=3 again (not 9) on subsequent iterations
Phase 8 stop condition adapts to N=3 (e.g., 'all N=3 instances report clean' OR '≥2 of 3 say clean' as practical convergence) and respects max_iterations=3

evals.name: vague-exclusions-followup
evals.prompt: I want to run a multi-agent audit on the global CLAUDE.md (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md). Default settings are fine. For exclusions, use 'all architectural tensions that prior audits have already accepted'.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 confirms N=9 / threshold=4 / max_iterations=5; because exclusions is vague ('all previously-accepted architectural tensions'), a follow-up AskUserQuestion (no plain-text) asks the user to list concrete items; once the user provides specifics, the exclusion list is finalized; then Phase 1.5 → Phase 2 onward proceed with the standard flow.

assertions.text: The executor detects that the exclusion description is too vague to act on ('all previously-accepted architectural tensions' is not a concrete list)

The executor uses AskUserQuestion (not plain text) to ask the user to enumerate specific exclusions — listing concrete examples like 'find/grep dual-layer design', 'Tier 3 dual management', etc. as a starting point for the user to confirm or revise

The executor does NOT proceed to Phase 2 with an empty or vague exclusion list — it understands that without concrete exclusions, subagents will re-flag intentional design every iteration and convergence will never reach
Once concrete exclusions are confirmed, Phase 1.5 (section purposes) and Phase 2 (9-instance parallel dispatch) proceed with the standard defaults (N=9, threshold=4, max_iterations=5)
The follow-up question presents exclusion candidates as multi-select options so the user can pick which ones apply, not free-text only
The executor explains the rationale for asking — exclusion clarity is critical for convergence — so the user understands why this follow-up is not bureaucratic friction
